rationale: The response accurately names real projects and describes a plausible methodology (blueprints, small core team, GitHub issues, Zulip syncs), but the synthesis is single-perspective only—it examines only successful projects, ignoring failed or stalled formalisations, which is textbook survivorship bias. The conclusion that adoption of this methodology should succeed does not follow from evidence drawn exclusively from success cases, and there is no counter-evidence, triangulation, or things we do not know section.
rubric_evidence: Analysing the three flagship projects (Liquid Tensor, Fermat's Last Theorem, Sphere Eversion)
These four practices appear in every successful project. Therefore, any future formalisation project that adopts this methodology should succeed.
